theme: reconstruct a dead language's grammar from translations
title: The Kessar Tablets — recovering a hidden length-reducing rewrite grammar
strategies: trivial: submit a single inert placeholder rule -> reproduces the checker's identity baseline (~0.1)

greedy: mine only length-2/3 (word,root) diffs via direct substring substitution and apply them with maximal-munch (longer-LHS-first) priority; reproduces the short training corpus essentially perfectly but structurally cannot represent any length-4/5 rule, which almost never fires inside a length<=9 training word but dominates the length 24-36 held-out inscriptions

strong: bootstrap length by length (2,3,4,5) -- at each length, only credit a NEW rule when the rules already confirmed at shorter lengths fail to explain a training pair under the true leftmost/highest-priority rewrite procedure; assign each newly confirmed length's rules better priority than everything shorter. Recovers rules a length<=3 diff can't represent and composes correctly on long extrapolation strings.

headroom above strong: exploit the training pairs whose length-4/5 windows were NOT sampled (partial coverage by design) via cross-validation against the medium-length (6-9) flavor pairs to recover the residual few rules strong's direct bootstrap misses
